news.title: Caller ID app Truecaller hits 500 million monthly users
news.summary: Truecaller now has more than 4 million paying users across the world.

news.title: Social gaming platform Rec Room, once valued at $3.5B, is shutting down
news.summary: Rec Room, the social platform for user-generated games and virtual experiences, will shutter on June 1.

news.title: Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
news.summary: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.

news.title: Whoop’s valuation just tripled to $10 billion
news.summary: The fitness tracking startup just closed a $575 million Series G with Cristiano Ronaldo and LeBron James among its investors. The obvious question looming over a round of this size at this valuation: Is an IPO coming?

news.title: Uber increases stake in WeRide as robotaxi partnership ramps up in Dubai
news.summary: Uber and WeRide have launched robotaxi operations without a human safety operator in Dubai as part of a broader expansion in the Middle East.

news.title: Evercade’s new handheld has a larger screen and dual thumbsticks for 3D games
news.summary: After releasing smaller versions of its handhelds, Evercade has announced a new portable console called the Nexus featuring a large 5.89-inch screen. news.summary: If you’re keen to get a new 4K TV that has built-in streaming services provided by Amazon’s Fire TV software, it’s tough to get more for your money than with Hisense’s U6. news.summary: After a couple of quiet years, Sonos is back with two new consumer speakers: the portable Sonos Play and the more affordable Era 100 SL. news.title: New quantum-computing advances heighten threat to elliptic curve cryptosystems
news.summary: No, the sky isn't falling, but Q Day is coming, and it won't be as expensive as thought.

news.title: You can finally change the goofy Gmail address you chose years ago
news.summary: All your data remains intact, and you can go back to your original address at any time.

news.title: OkCupid gave 3 million dating-app photos to facial recognition firm, FTC says
news.summary: OkCupid and Match settle with Trump FTC, don't have to pay any financial penalty.

news.title: This is my third Orion launch, but it feels totally different
news.summary: The first two launches of Orion felt hollow, but NASA is finally on a better course.

news.title: Costco sued for seeking refunds on tariffs customers paid
news.summary: Proposed class action accuses Costco of unjust enrichment.

news.title: Our Favorite Affordable Air Purifier Is Temporarily Even Cheaper
news.summary: Amazon has the Coway Airmega Mighty marked down to two-thirds its normal price.

news.title: Google Now Lets You Change Your Gmail Address. Here’s How
news.summary: You’ve probably had the same Gmail address for years. Now, it’s easy to make a name change without worrying about the transition.

news.title: The US Military’s GPS Software Is an $8 Billion Mess
news.summary: The GPS Next-Generation Operational Control System was due for completion in 2016. Ten years later, the software for controlling the military’s GPS satellites still doesn’t work.

news.title: The Promise of 'Woke 2' Is Fueling a Leftist Fever Dream
news.summary: Progressives are dreaming about a new political order that rights the wrongs of the Trump administration and the shortcomings of “Woke 1.” Does it have a shot?

news.title: Tesla Admits Its Robotaxis Are Sometimes Driven by Remote Humans
news.summary: The electric-car maker says it happens rarely and at speeds below 10 mph. But the disclosure—in response to a US senator's questions—occasioned a call for more transparency.
